name: Viral Infection Common Cold
description: The common cold is a very frequent viral infection in India, particularly during weather transitions and monsoons. It primarily affects the upper respiratory tract.
self_care: Rest, stay hydrated with warm water, and try steam inhalation. Use over-the-counter pain relievers or throat lozenges if needed.

name: Influenza Flu
description: Influenza is a common respiratory illness in India that spreads easily during seasonal changes. It causes a sudden onset of high fever and severe body aches.
self_care: Get plenty of rest and drink fluids. Take paracetamol for fever and body aches.

name: Dengue Fever
description: Dengue is a viral infection transmitted by mosquitoes, very common during India's monsoon season. It leads to high fever, severe headache, and joint pains.
self_care: Paracetamol is recommended for pain and fever; strictly avoid ibuprofen and aspirin. Stay highly hydrated and monitor platelet counts if advised by a doctor.

name: Malaria
description: A parasitic infection transmitted by mosquitoes, frequently seen in specific parts of India during the rainy season. It brings cyclical fever accompanied by severe shivering.
self_care: Keep warm during chills and wipe with a cool cloth when fever spikes. Hydrate continuously.

name: Typhoid Fever
description: Typhoid is a bacterial infection prevalent in parts of India where water or food hygiene is compromised. Prolonged fever and stomach issues are key symptoms.
self_care: Drink only boiled or purified water. Eat plain, bland easily digestible foods like khichdi and boiled vegetables.

name: Chickenpox
description: A highly contagious viral infection that causes an itchy, blister-like rash all over the body. It commonly affects children but can occur in unvaccinated adults.
self_care: Apply calamine lotion to soothe itching and take cool baths. Avoid scratching the blisters to prevent scarring.

name: Bronchitis
description: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes, often exacerbated by high pollution levels and winter smog in Indian cities. Triggers a persistent cough that may bring up mucus.
self_care: Inhale steam with eucalyptus oil and drink warm fluids like ginger tea. Avoid exposure to smoke and dust or cold air.

name: Asthma Attack
description: A sudden worsening of asthma symptoms, often triggered by dust mites, smog, or allergens present in urban environments. It causes severe difficulty in breathing.
self_care: Sit upright and use your prescribed rescue inhaler immediately. Focus on taking slow, calm breaths.

name: Pneumonia
description: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid. Represents a severe lung infection that demands medical care.
self_care: Ensure plenty of rest to support your immune system. Use extra pillows at night to help ease breathing.

name: Angina Chest Pain
description: A type of chest pain caused by reduced blood flow to the heart, feeling like squeezing or pressure. A critical symptom mapping to cardiovascular conditions.
self_care: Stop all physical activity safely and rest. If you have prescribed emergency medicine like sorbitrate, take it as directed.

name: Cardiac Arrhythmia
description: Changes in the regular beating of the heart, resulting in sudden racing, fluttering, or pounding sensations. Can be benign but warrants a complete evaluation.
self_care: Stay calm, sit comfortably, and try deep breathing or meditation. Avoid caffeine, alcohol, or heavy exertion.

name: Gastroenteritis
description: Commonly known as stomach flu or food poisoning, often caused by contaminated food sourced outside. It results in stomach upset, frequent loose stools, and vomiting.
self_care: Rehydrate with ORS (oral rehydration salts), nimbu pani, and coconut water. Progress to dry toast and light meals as tolerated.

name: Acid Reflux GERD
description: A chronic disease that occurs when stomach acid or bile flows back into the food pipe. Often aggravated by heavily spiced Indian dietary habits or irregular meals.
self_care: Avoid lying down immediately after eating and keep your head elevated. Consume cold milk and reduce intake of spicy, oily foods.

name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome
description: A common systemic disorder affecting the large intestine, with symptoms triggered heavily by erratic dietary habits or stress. Causes alternating diarrhea and constipation.
self_care: Track what triggers symptoms and practice portion control. Yoga and mindful breathing can help manage stress factors.

name: Migraine
description: A severe headache condition marked by a pulsing sensation, usually on one side of the head. Light and sounds can exacerbate the intense headache.
self_care: Rest in a quiet, completely dark room. Apply cold or warm compresses to the head or neck area.

name: Tension Headache
description: The most common form of a headache, producing a dull, aching pain or tightness across the forehead or neck. Very common among working professionals.
self_care: Perform gentle neck stretches and use ergonomic seating. Gently massage your temples.

name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
description: An autoimmune, inflammatory disease that causes the immune system to mistakenly attack your joints. Causes chronic pain, stiffness, and joint deformities.
self_care: Apply hot or cold compress as needed to reduce swelling. Maintain light joint mobility exercises like walking.

name: Muscle Strain
description: Overstretching or tearing injuries to a muscle or tendon. They are extremely common during sudden physical activity or incorrect lifting posture.
self_care: Apply the RICE protocol: Rest, Ice, Compression, and Elevation. Discontinue aggravating physical activity completely for a few days.

name: Allergic Reaction
description: An overly sensitive immune response to a harmless substance (food, insect bites, or medications). Severity can range from minor rashes to severe breathing issues.
self_care: Take immediate over-the-counter antihistamines. Avoid the suspected allergen and seek immediate help if breathing worsens.

name: Eczema Atopic Dermatitis
description: A condition causing skin to become red, inflamed, and intensely itchy. Regular exposure to extreme heat, cold or strong detergents triggers the condition.
self_care: Moisturize the skin continuously with basic unscented creams. Wear breathable clothing like cotton.
